title: cathartolinum catharticum
intro: Cathartolinum catharticum, also known as the "purging daisy," is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, traditionally used for its medicinal and purgative properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its cathartic effects, helping to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. In herbal medicine, Cathartolinum catharticum is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Cathartolinum catharticum can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
